title: Proficient trigonometrical-fitted two-derivative multistep collocation methods in predictor-corrector approach: Application to perturbed Kepler problem
author: Lee, Aris, et al.
description: An efficient trigonometrical-fitted two-derivative multistep collocation (TF-TDMC) method using Legendre polynomials up to order five as the basis functions, has been developed for solving second-order ordinary differential equations with oscillatory solution effectively. Interpolation method of approximated power series and collocation technique of its second and third derivative are implemented in the construction of the methods. Two-derivative multistep collocation methods are developed in predictor and corrector form with varying collocation and interpolation points. Later, trigonometrically-fitting technique is implemented into TF-TDMC method, using the linear combination of trigonometrical functions, to produce frequency-dependent coefficients in TF-TDMC method. The stability of the TF-TDMC method, with fitted parameters, is thoroughly analyzed and has been proven to achieve zero stability. Stability polynomials and regions for predictor and corrector of TF-TDMC method are developed and plotted. In the operation of the TF-TDMC method, initial conditions and the frequency for each problem (based on the exact solutions) are identified. The frequency-dependent coefficients are then adjusted according to the identified frequency. Predictor and corrector steps are implemented to estimate and refine the values of the dependent variable and its derivative, ensuring that convergence is achieved.A numerical experiment demonstrates that the proposed method significantly outperforms other existing methods in the literature, achieving the lowest maximum global error with moderate computational time across all step sizes for solving second-order ordinary differential equations with oscillatory solutions. Additionally, it effectively addresses real-world perturbed Kepler problems. The results include a detailed discussion and analysis of the numerical performance.ŌĆóAn efficient two-derivative multistep collocation method in predictor-corrector mode with trigonometrically-fitting technique (TF-TDMC) is developed for direct solving second-order ordinary differential equations with oscillatory solution.ŌĆóTF-TDMC method has been proved to acquire zero-stability and its stability region is analyzed.ŌĆóTF-TDMC method is the best among all selected methods in solving second-order ordinary differential equations with oscillatory solution, including perturbed Kepler problem.

steps_raw.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps_raw.instruction: In this study, we plan to construct efficient fifth-order two-derivative multistep collocation (TDMC) method with predictor-corrector mode to solve special class of second-order ordinary differential equation in the form of(1)yŌĆ│(x)=f(x,y(x)),y(x0)=y0,yŌĆ▓(x0)=yŌĆ▓0.

steps_raw.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps_raw.instruction: We begin with the general formulation of TDMC method for solving problem (1), comprises the derivative of the solution yŌĆ│ŌĆ▓(x)=g(x,y(x),yŌĆ▓(x))=gx(x,y(x),yŌĆ▓(x))+gy(x,y(x),yŌĆ▓(x))yŌĆ▓+gyŌĆ▓(x,y(x),yŌĆ▓(x))f as follows:(2)Ōłæj=0k╬▒jy(x+jh)+Ōłæj=0m1╬▒vjy(x+vjh)=h2(Ōłæj=0k╬▓jfn+j+Ōłæj=0m2╬▓vjfn+vj)+h3(Ōłæj=0k╬│jgn+j+Ōłæj=0m3╬│vjgn+vj),where ╬▒j,╬▒vj,╬▓j,╬▓vj,╬│j,╬│vjŌłłR,m1,m2,m3,kŌłłZ+ and vj is non-integer.

steps_raw.instruction: To construct TDMC method, we approximate the solution by employing the interpolating function with Legendre polynomials serving as basis functions,(3)T0=1,T1=z,T2=32z2ŌłÆ12,T3=52z3ŌłÆ32z,T4=358z4ŌłÆ154z2+38,T5=638z5ŌłÆ354z3+158z.

steps_raw.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps_raw.instruction: Then, we approximate the solution using an interpolating function (power series) in the following equation:(4)Y(x)=Ōłæj=0s1+s2+╬ĘŌłÆ1ajTj(x),where xŌłł[x0,b],aj is unknown parameter, Tj is Legendre polynomial, s1 and s2 are the number of interpolations for second and third derivative respectively, ╬Ę is distinct collocation point with ╬Ę>0 and b is endpoints of interval.

steps_raw.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps_raw.instruction: Similarly, the second derivative of third derivative of interpolating function will be as follows:YŌĆ│(x)=Ōłæj=0s1+s2+╬ĘŌłÆ1ajTŌĆ│j(x),(5)YŌĆ│ŌĆ▓(x)=Ōłæj=0s1+s2+╬ĘŌłÆ1ajTŌĆ│ŌĆ▓j(x).

steps_raw.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps_raw.instruction: The continuous approximation is formulated by enforcing the following conditions:(6)Y(xn+╬Ę)=yn+╬Ę,╬Ę=0,1,53,YŌĆ│(xn+s1)=fn+s1,s1=0,1,53,2,YŌĆ│ŌĆ▓(xn+s2)=gn+s2,s2=0,1,53,2.

steps_raw.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps_raw.instruction: At first, we develop predictor formulae for TDMC method, we solve three set of equations based on (4) and (5) to obtain the predictor based on the grid points, 53 and 2. We solve Y(xn),Y(xn+1),YŌĆ│(xn),YŌĆ│(xn+1),YŌĆ│ŌĆ▓(xn),YŌĆ│ŌĆ▓(xn+1) simultaneously to obtain coefficients aj,j=0,1,...,5, then substituting the values aj into Eq. (4) and yield the continuous method as follows:(7)Y(x)=Ōłæ01╬▒jyn+j+h2Ōłæ01╬▓jfn+j+h3Ōłæ01╬│jgn+j.

steps_raw.instruction: Therefore, the complete formula for the corrector is provided below:(15)yn+53=ŌłÆ23yn+53yn+1+h2(79486fn+191486fn+1)+h3(11243gn+118gn+1),hyŌĆ▓n+53=ŌłÆyn+yn+1+h2(8831620fn+10071620fn+1)+h3(49270gn+6791620gn+1),yn+2=ŌłÆ12yn+1+32yn+53+h2(124fn+1+18fn+53)+h3(1144gn+1+1432gn+53),hyŌĆ▓n+2=ŌłÆ32yn+1+32yn+53+h2(49240fn+1+3780fn+53)+h3(29720gn+1+1672160gn+53),

steps_raw.title: Trigonometrically-Fitted two-derivative linear multistep method
steps_raw.instruction: In developing the TDMC method with a trigonometric fitting technique, denoted as TF-TDMC method, we replace some coefficients in TDMC method with Ai and Bi,i=1,2,...,6 as follows:(17)yn+53=ŌłÆ23yn+53yn+1+h2(A1fn+191486fn+1)+h3(A2gn+118gn+1),yn+2=ŌłÆ12yn+1+32yn+53+h2(124fn+1+A3fn+53)+h3(1144gn+1+A4gn+53),hyŌĆ▓n+53=ŌłÆyn+yn+1+h2(B1fn+10071620fn+1)+h3(B2gn+6791620gn+1),hyŌĆ▓n+2=ŌłÆ32yn+1+32yn+53+h2(49240fn+1+B3fn+53)+h3(29720gn+1+B4gn+53),(18)yn+2=ŌłÆ12yn+1+32yn+5/3+h2(A5fn+5/3+512fn+2)+h3(A6gn+5/3ŌłÆ118gn+2),hyŌĆ▓n+2=ŌłÆ32yn+1+32yn+5/3+h2(B5fn+5/3+4130fn+2)+h3(B6gn+5/3ŌłÆ31180gn+2).

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Here, we discuss the zero stability of block collocation method for solving second-order ODEs defined by [8] as follows:

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Definition 1(Zero stable) A block multistep method with order p is zero stable provided the roots, Rl for l=0,1,...,m of the characteristic polynomial, Žā(R) such that:(24)Žā(R)=det[Ōłæl=0mP(l)R(mŌłÆl)],P(0)=I, satisfy |Rl|Ōēż1 for l=0,1,...,m. If Rl is a repeated root, then the multiplicity of the root of modulus 1 must be at most 2, where I is identity matrix and P(l) is m├Śm matrix.

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: First, we use second-order test problem as follow:(25)yŌĆ│=ŌłÆ╬Ė2y,╬Ė>0.

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Apply predictor formulae of TF-TDMC method into the test problem above and substitute w=╬Ėh, we obtain(26)yn+53=(ŌłÆ23ŌłÆA1w2)yn+(53ŌłÆ191486w2)yn+1+(ŌłÆA2w2)hyŌĆ▓n+(ŌłÆ118w2)hyŌĆ▓n+1,(27)hyŌĆ▓n+53=(ŌłÆ1ŌłÆB1w2)yn+(1ŌłÆ10071620w2)yn+1+(ŌłÆB2w2)hyŌĆ▓n+(ŌłÆ6791620w2)hyŌĆ▓n+1,(28)yn+2=(ŌłÆ12ŌłÆ124w2)yn+1+(32ŌłÆA3w2)yn+53+(ŌłÆ1144w2)hyŌĆ▓n+1+(ŌłÆA4w2)hyŌĆ▓n+53,(29)hyŌĆ▓n+2=(ŌłÆ32ŌłÆ49240w2)yn+1+(32ŌłÆB3w2)yn+53+(ŌłÆ1144w2)hyŌĆ▓n+1+(ŌłÆB4w2)hyŌĆ▓n+53.

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: We substitute (yn+2hyŌĆ▓n+2)=R2,(yn+1hyŌĆ▓n+1)=R and (ynhyŌĆ▓n)=1, we yield the following first characteristic polynomial(32)Žā(R,w)=(1001)R2ŌłÆ(M11M12M21M22)RŌłÆ(N11N12N21N22).

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Then we determine the determinant of the first characteristic polynomial and set w=0. This results in the following stability polynomial:(33)R4ŌłÆ2R3+R2=0.

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Hence, the roots of stability polynomial are 0,0,1,1. All of the roots have modulus less or equal to one, which satisfied the zero stable conditions given in Definition 1. Thus, we conclude that the predictor formulae of TF-TDMC method is zero stable.

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Next, we let Žā(R,w)=0 and solve for R in term of w within the matrice, resulting in 2├Ś2 matrix P(w). The stability region in complex plane of TF-TDMC method can be defined as(34)RS={w:|╬╗i(P(w))|<1,i=1,2},where ╬╗i are eigenvalues of P(w). The stability region of predictor for TF-TDMC method is shown in Fig. 1.Fig. 1The stability region of predictor for TF-TDMC method.Fig 1

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Similarly, for analyzing corrector of TF-TDMC method, we can simply transform into the matrix form:(35)(1+512w2)yn+2=(ŌłÆ12)yn+1+(32ŌłÆA5w2)yn+53+(ŌłÆA6w2)hyŌĆ▓n+53,(36)(1ŌłÆ31180w2)hyŌĆ▓n+2=(ŌłÆ32)yn+1+(32ŌłÆB5w2)yn+53+(ŌłÆ4130w2)yn+2+(ŌłÆB6w2)hyŌĆ▓n+53.

steps_raw.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Using the similar way, we determinant of first characteristic polynomial and set w=0. Stability polynomial is similar as Eq. (32), the roots of stability polynomial are 0,0,1,1. All the roots have modulus less or equal to one as well. It means that the corrector formulae of TF-TDMC method is zero stable. Both predictor and corrector method of TF-TDMC method are zero-stable. The stability region of corrector for TF-TDMC method is shown in Fig. 2.Fig. 2The stability region of corrector for TF-TDMC method.Fig 2

steps_raw.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Step 1 Initialization

steps_raw.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Start with an initial value for the dependent variable and its derivative, along with the initial conditions and step size, h for the problem. Identify the frequency, ╬Ė of the problem and subsequently, determine the frequency-dependent parameters to be used in the predictor and corrector steps of the TF-TDMC method with the information of w=╬Ėh.Step 2 Predictor Step

steps_raw.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Use two-step predictor, based on previous values, to estimate the dependent variable, yn+k and its derivative, yŌĆ▓n+k, k=53,2.Step 3 Corrector Step

steps_raw.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Use the predicted values in a corrector formula to refine the estimates of. yn+2 and yŌĆ▓n+2 with tolerance of 10ŌłÆ20. Check for convergence by comparing the predictor and corrector estimates, or by comparing the previous term with the current term in the corrector step. If they are sufficiently close, proceed; if not, iterate until convergence is achieved.Step 4 Iteration

steps_raw.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Repeat the predictor and corrector steps for subsequent time steps, using the updated values from the corrector step as the starting point for the next iteration until reach to the endpoints.Step 5 Output

steps_raw.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps_raw.instruction: Once the desired accuracy is achieved, output the values of the dependent variable at the specified time steps.
